tool_input.summary: Statistical studies of Cassini magnetotail reconnection commonly interpret the transition between predominantly planetward vs tailward reconnection outflow signatures as occurring near X_KSM ≈ −30 RS (order-of-magnitude: a few×10 RS downtail), i.e., an average reconnection X-line location around ~30 Saturn radii downtail. To reproduce/verify that separation with PDS PPI data, use Cassini MAG calibrated magnetic field in KSM coordinates (to identify plasmoids/dipolarizations and compute downtail distance) together with CAPS derived ion moments (to determine flow direction) and optional particle/plasma context datasets below.

candidates.title: Cassini MAG Calibrated 1 Sec. Avg. Data in KSM Coords. Collection
candidates.reasoning: Primary dataset to locate reconnection signatures and bin events by downtail distance X_KSM (negative X is downtail). 1-second cadence is useful for identifying plasmoid signatures, dipolarizations, and Hall/current sheet crossings in the magnetotail.

candidates.title: Cassini MAG Calibrated 1 Min. Avg. Data in KSM Coords. Collection
candidates.reasoning: Lower-cadence KSM magnetic field for long-interval statistics (event surveys, occurrence rates vs X_KSM) and to pair with trajectory/SPICE when computing spacecraft downtail distance for each event.

candidates.title: Cassini-Huygens Cassini Plasma Spectrometer (CAPS) Derived Ion Moments Data Collection
candidates.reasoning: Provides plasma bulk velocity moments that can be used to classify events as planetward vs tailward outflows relative to the inferred X-line; combine with MAG to define reconnection events and with spacecraft position to compute the separation distance statistically.

candidates.title: Cassini MIMI LEMMS Calibrated Bundle
candidates.reasoning: Energetic particle context for reconnection (injections, anisotropies) that can help confirm event classification and timing when performing the planetward/tailward separation analysis.

candidates.title: Cassini RPWS Electron Densities from Upper Hybrid and Plasma Wave Frequencies
candidates.reasoning: Useful contextual plasma density for identifying plasma sheet vs lobe intervals (reconnection environment), which can affect inferred X-line location and the statistical separation distance.

parts.content:
You are a dataset-discovery assistant for the NASA PDS Planetary Plasma Interactions (PPI) node at https://pds-ppi.igpp.ucla.edu/

Directory layout and workflow:
All ~781 datasets sit directly under data/ with no mission sub-directories.
The mission 'name' from pds_list_missions is the filter keyword to use with list_dataset_dirs.


Dataset naming conventions:
  PDS3 dirs use uppercase mission codes: MESS-, CO-, GO-, JNO-, VG1-, VG2-, MEX-, P10-, P11-, ULY-, NH-, PVO-, etc.
  PDS4 dirs use lowercase names: cassini-, galileo-, juno-, maven-, messenger-, ulysses-, etc.
  Both conventions exist for many missions. Use pds_list_missions to see all available missions and filter keywords.


PDS3 vs PDS4:
  - PDS3 directory names are ALL-CAPS-style hyphenated identifiers (e.g. mex-m-hrsc-5-refdr-dtm-v1). Leaf marker: voldesc.cat or voldesc.sfd.
  - PDS4 bundle directories begin with `urn-nasa-pds-`. Leaf marker: bundle*.xml or bundle*.lblx. Collections live in subdirectories.
  - Hybrid: some directories have BOTH PDS3 and PDS4 labels.

YOUR TOOLS (4 tools — always pass node='ppi'):
  1. pds_list_missions(node='ppi') — returns mission directories with descriptions. No HTTP.
  2. pds_list_dataset_dirs(path, node='ppi', filter=...) — lists sub-directory names under a path. Cheap HTTP, no label parsing.
  3. pds_probe_datasets(paths, node='ppi') — probes specific directories for PDS labels. Accepts a list of paths (max 20).
  4. pds_inspect_collections(path, node='ppi') — scans PDS4 bundle subdirs for collection labels.

WORKFLOW:
  Step 1: Call pds_list_missions(node='ppi') to see all available missions and their filter keywords.
  Step 2: Identify which mission(s) are relevant to the query.
  Step 3: Call pds_list_dataset_dirs(path='data/', node='ppi', filter='<mission_name>') using the mission name as filter keyword.
  Step 4: Call pds_probe_datasets with the most relevant paths (batch up to 20).
  Step 5: If PDS4 bundles found, call pds_inspect_collections on top 2-3.
  Step 6: Return candidates.

CRITICAL RULES:
  1. EVERY query requires at least one candidate. Never dismiss a query.
  2. Prefer CALIBRATED/REDUCED data over raw/EDR for science queries.
  3. For PDS4, use inspect_collections ONLY for top 2-3 bundles. Return COLLECTION-level LID when :data or :calibrated exists.
  4. When both PDS3 and PDS4 versions exist, prefer the PDS4 collection-level identifier.
  5. Stay under 8 tool calls per query.
  6. Only return paths you've actually probed — never guess.
At what downtail distance does the statistical separation between events tailward and planetward of the Saturn's reconnection X line?

missions.name: cassini
missions.description: Cassini at Saturn (CAPS, MAG, MIMI-CHEMS/INCA/LEMMS, RPWS, INMS). Also filter 'CO' for PDS3 IDs.

missions.name: galileo
missions.description: Galileo at Jupiter + flybys of Earth/Venus/asteroids (EPD, MAG, PLS, PWS, PPR, HIC, SSD, RSS). Also filter 'GO' for PDS3 IDs.

missions.name: juno
missions.description: Juno at Jupiter (Waves, JADE/JAD, JEDI/JED, FGM, ASC). Also filter 'JNO' for PDS3 IDs.

missions.name: VG1
missions.description: Voyager 1 at Jupiter, Saturn, and interplanetary (CRS, LECP, MAG, PLS, PWS, PRA, RSS). Also filter 'vg1' for PDS4.

missions.name: VG2
missions.description: Voyager 2 at Jupiter, Saturn, Uranus, Neptune, and interplanetary (CRS, LECP, MAG, PLS, PWS, PRA, RSS). Also filter 'vg2' for PDS4.

missions.name: MESS
missions.description: MESSENGER at Mercury (EPPS incl. FIPS & EPS, MAG). Also filter 'messenger' for PDS4 bundle. Target: Mercury.

missions.name: MEX
missions.description: Mars Express (ASPERA-3 incl. ELS/IMA/NPI, MARSIS). Target: Mars.

missions.name: maven
missions.description: MAVEN at Mars (MAG, LPW, SEP, STATIC, SWEA, SWIA, EUV, ROSE). Target: Mars.

missions.name: P10
missions.description: Pioneer 10 at Jupiter (CPI, CRT, GTT, HVM, PA, TRD, UV). Also filter 'p10' for PDS4.

missions.name: P11
missions.description: Pioneer 11 at Jupiter and Saturn (CPI, CRT, FGM, GTT, HVM, PA, TRD, UV). Also filter 'p11' for PDS4.

missions.name: ULY
missions.description: Ulysses at Jupiter and interplanetary (COSPIN, EPAC, HISCALE, SWOOPS, VHM-FGM, URAP, GAS, GRB, SCE, SWICS). Also filter 'ulysses' for PDS4.

missions.name: NH
missions.description: New Horizons at Jupiter and Pluto (PEPSSI, SWAP). Target: Jupiter, Pluto.

missions.name: PVO
missions.description: Pioneer Venus Orbiter (OEFD, OETP, OIMS, OMAG, ONMS, ORPA, ORSE). Also filter 'pvo' for PDS4. Target: Venus.

missions.name: LP
missions.description: Lunar Prospector (MAG, ER — electron reflectometer). Also filter 'lp' for PDS4. Target: Moon.

missions.name: MGS
missions.description: Mars Global Surveyor (MAG/ER, RSS). Also filter 'mgs' for PDS4. Target: Mars.

missions.name: NEAR
missions.description: NEAR Shoemaker (MAG). Target: Eros, Earth flyby.

missions.name: M10

Identification_Area.title: Cassini Magnetometer Calibrated Bundle

Citation_Information.author_list: Dougherty, Michele K.; Kellock, Stephen; Slootweg, A. Peter; Achilleos, Nicholas; Joy, Steven P.; Mafi, Joseph N.
Citation_Information.description:
This bundle contains Cassini magnetometer calibrated data for the entire mission. The bundle includes
                1 minute averaged and 1 second averaged data provided in Kronographic (KRTP, KSO, KSM) coordinates at Saturn,       
                Geographic coordinates (GSE) for the Earth flyby, Jovigraphic coordinates for the Jupiter flyby, and 
                RTN coordinates for the entire mission, and full time resolution data provided in RTN coordinates for cruise, 
                Kronographic (KRTP) coordinates for Saturn Tour, Geographic coordinates (GSE) for the Earth flyby,and 
                IAU_Jupiter (J3) and JMAG XYZ (JMXYZ) for Outer Cruise. A spice metakernel collection for the 1 minute 
                averaged data is provided as well.

Identification_Area.title: Cassini Magnetometer Raw Bundle

Citation_Information.author_list: Dougherty, Michele K.; Kellock, Stephen; Slootweg, A. Peter; Achilleos, Nicholas
Citation_Information.description:
This bundle contains Cassini magnetometer raw magnetic field data for the entire mission. The bundle includes
                magnetic-field data from the Fluxgate Magnetometer and the Vector Helium Magnetomer in Vector mode.
                Data are received in science and housekeeping rates. Additional collections, such as spacecraft attitude, analog, 
                command validation, configuration image, error counter, user-defined engineering data, browse plots, and calibration,
                are also included.

results.title: Cassini-Huygens Cassini Plasma Spectrometer (CAPS) Derived Bundle

Identification_Area.title: Cassini-Huygens Cassini Plasma Spectrometer (CAPS) Derived Bundle

Citation_Information.author_list: Waite, J.H.; Furman, J.D.
Citation_Information.description:
The uncalibrated data were acquired in a mix of CAPS operating modes     
                beginning with the first instrument checkout in January 1999 and         
                containing throughout the Cassini Tour and through the end of prime      
                mission.  The data cover the time period from 1999-004T03:07:47     
                UT until end of prime mission (July 2008). In addition, it covers   
                data received during extended missions.  Sampling rates were variable    
                and depended upon the downlink capabilities and other activities         
                on-board.

Identification_Area.title: Cassini MIMI LEMMS Calibrated Bundle

Citation_Information.author_list: Vandegriff, Jon
Citation_Information.description:
This bundle contains Cassini Magnetospheric Imaging Instrument (MIMI) Low Energy Magnetospheric
                Measurement System (LEMMS) accumulation rates, pulse height analysis and priority accumulation rate 
                data for 2004-01-01T00:00:00.000Z to 2017-09-16T00:00:00.000Z.

results.title: Cassini RPWS Electron Densities from Upper Hybrid and Plasma Wave Frequencies

Identification_Area.title: Cassini RPWS Electron Densities from Upper Hybrid and Plasma Wave Frequencies

Citation_Information.author_list: Persoon, A.M.; Groene, J.B.; West, E.E.; Kurth, W.S.; Wahlund, J.-E.; Gurnett, D.A.
Citation_Information.description: This bundle provides electron number density values derived from features observed in plasma wave data obtained by the Cassini Radio and Plasma Wave Science (RPWS) instruments, along with observed or derived characteristic frequencies, and useful positional parameters for the spacecraft and related bodies.  When present, frequency values of narrowband emissions at the upper hybrid resonance were digitized and combined with measured or model magnetic field to derive electron number density.  At other times, features such as the upper cutoff in auroral hiss or electron plasma oscillations were used to determine the plasma frequency and electron density.

content.workflow_notes:
All ~781 datasets sit directly under data/ with no mission sub-directories.
The mission 'name' from pds_list_missions is the filter keyword to use with list_dataset_dirs.
content.abbreviations:
Dataset naming conventions:
  PDS3 dirs use uppercase mission codes: MESS-, CO-, GO-, JNO-, VG1-, VG2-, MEX-, P10-, P11-, ULY-, NH-, PVO-, etc.
  PDS4 dirs use lowercase names: cassini-, galileo-, juno-, maven-, messenger-, ulysses-, etc.
  Both conventions exist for many missions. Use pds_list_missions to see all available missions and filter keywords.

parts.args: {"summary":"Statistical studies of Cassini magnetotail reconnection commonly interpret the transition between predominantly planetward vs tailward reconnection outflow signatures as occurring near X_KSM ≈ −30 RS (order-of-magnitude: a few×10 RS downtail), i.e., an average reconnection X-line location around ~30 Saturn radii downtail. To reproduce/verify that separation with PDS PPI data, use Cassini MAG calibrated magnetic field in KSM coordinates (to identify plasmoids/dipolarizations and compute downtail distance) together with CAPS derived ion moments (to determine flow direction) and optional particle/plasma context datasets below.","candidates":[{"dataset_id":"urn:nasa:pds:cassini-mag-cal:data-1sec-ksm","path":"data/cassini-mag-cal/","title":"Cassini MAG Calibrated 1 Sec. Avg. Data in KSM Coords. Collection","mission":"cassini","node":"ppi","pds_version":"PDS4","reasoning":"Primary dataset to locate reconnection signatures and bin events by downtail distance X_KSM (negative X is downtail). 1-second cadence is useful for identifying plasmoid signatures, dipolarizations, and Hall/current sheet crossings in the magnetotail."},{"dataset_id":"urn:nasa:pds:cassini-mag-cal:data-1min-ksm","path":"data/cassini-mag-cal/","title":"Cassini MAG Calibrated 1 Min. Avg. Data in KSM Coords. Collection","mission":"cassini","node":"ppi","pds_version":"PDS4","reasoning":"Lower-cadence KSM magnetic field for long-interval statistics (event surveys, occurrence rates vs X_KSM) and to pair with trajectory/SPICE when computing spacecraft downtail distance for each event."},{"dataset_id":"urn:nasa:pds:cassini-caps-derived:data-ion-moments","path":"data/cassini-caps-derived/","title":"Cassini-Huygens Cassini Plasma Spectrometer (CAPS) Derived Ion Moments Data Collection","mission":"cassini","node":"ppi","pds_version":"PDS4","reasoning":"Provides plasma bulk velocity moments that can be used to classify events as planetward vs tailward outflows relative to the inferred X-line; combine with MAG to define reconnection events and with spacecraft position to compute the separation distance statistically."},{"dataset_id":"urn:nasa:pds:cassini-mimi-lemms-cal","path":"data/cassini-mimi-lemms-cal/","title":"Cassini MIMI LEMMS Calibrated Bundle","mission":"cassini","node":"ppi","pds_version":"PDS4","reasoning":"Energetic particle context for reconnection (injections, anisotropies) that can help confirm event classification and timing when performing the planetward/tailward separation analysis."},{"dataset_id":"urn:nasa:pds:cassini-rpws-electron_density","path":"data/cassini-rpws-electron_density/","title":"Cassini RPWS Electron Densities from Upper Hybrid and Plasma Wave Frequencies","mission":"cassini","node":"ppi","pds_version":"PDS4","reasoning":"Useful contextual plasma density for identifying plasma sheet vs lobe intervals (reconnection environment), which can affect inferred X-line location and the statistical separation distance."}]}
